Oh, I realized, so when we were, when we started working together, we were actually in the process of replacing our old article generation pipelines with the new agentic ones.
And typically when we start working with a new client, I like to do like a walkthrough of our article generation process, but we weren't able to do that.

So this is the agentic pipeline.
It's still called testing, but it's no longer being tested, like we're using it.
So it consists of like, first of all, it's built on our in-house like content generation platform called Atlas.

So the first step is to give.
So
The platform, a topic.
This could be like the title.
So as you can see, like most of these have the title, but we can also provide just a primary keyword.
And then once we give it a primary keyword, we can also, we also try to provide it with like a very specific assignment direction.
We do this because we really want to control the outline.
Anybody who's been writing for long enough, you know that like the outline is really like the most important part of the article.
Our outline is a mess.
The article is going to be a mess.
So we try to make sure to give it a really good direction on like what are going, what are the sections going to be?
What do we need to provide in this article?
It has to like follow a very specific flow that is logical and like builds on top of each other and most importantly, like fulfills the promise of the title.
A lot of AI-generated outlines without any human input might actually not be related to the title all that much.
So we try to control this process as much as possible.
We give this very detailed assignment direction.
And then the first step is for the AI to research a specific topic.
So in this case, we are researching procurement finance.
And it's connected to, sorry, let me take, yeah, so it's connected to a researcher.
We use a mix of different research tools like perplexity, but we also use more advanced ones like EXA and Tavoli.
They are a lot more advanced.

of the information that we can find.

So, the way it researches a topic is that it takes the assignment direction, and then it uses AI to generate questions about that topic that it has to answer.
So, let me just...
So, we can actually inspect the process to take a look at the back end and what's happening behind the scenes.
So, the workflow is basically, okay, we're writing an article to cover this topic, procurement finance.
Here's a detailed outline.
So, it takes this outline, it analyzes it, it takes the section, and then it generates a research plan.
So, generates multiple questions.
For example, it first identifies, okay, who's the target audience here?
So, it understands, okay, our target audience, our chief procurement procurement officer.
So, for example, the first question that it generated is, like, what are the current industry benchmarks and statistics that we could find?
And it also has a success criteria for each of these queries, because once it collects the answers to these queries, it's also going to grade the answers.
And if the answers don't meet the success criteria bar, it's going to go back and try to find additional answers.
If it's not, if the research doesn't pass the criteria, it's not going to be used.
And then it also has...
something I've incorporated into mine, too.

And then, like, it synthesizes the results in this, like, very comprehensive doc.
And then it evaluates the research quality.
So, for each query, it gives it a score on a scale of one to zero.
It evaluates whether it meets the criteria.
And it also identifies any potential gaps.

And there's also...

In this case, it didn't have to generate any additional questions because all of the responses met the threshold.

or could not be able to access, so it can catch inaccurate information a lot faster, and what it will do is once it flags an inaccurate statement, it's going to go back in and rewrite a specific sentence, so for example, if we have a sentence that claims that this platform results in a specific outcome and it could not verify that claim, the sentence is going to be changed to, although like industries, although like there are, you know, people claim that this platform can result in this outcome, there are no like verifiable sources that support this claim, so while we're reading, we can actually spot that and be like, oh, that's the fact checker, then we can either remove that claim or try to like do some manual digging to see if we can replace the stat or or provide any other information, but it just makes our job easier, so this is for example, the fact checker.

## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content progress, review the generation process, and discuss future comms alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Publishing Blocked:** The "Direct vs. Indirect Procurement" article is ready but blocked by a pending Framer access request for the `pod zero` account.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** 4–6 articles are targeted for publishing by EOW/early next week, with Ben reviewing 6 articles (Status 4) and Sydney revising 2 (Status 5).](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)
  - [**Process Alignment:** GrowthX will adopt Ben's Gong-derived pain points and add direct links to analyst sources to improve content relevance and build relationships.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1176.0)
  - [**Comms Alignment:** A new monthly/quarterly "comms huddle" will align GrowthX, Gumshoe, and a new PR team on shared personas and topics.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1176.0)

## Topics

### Content Status & Pipeline

  - [**"Direct vs. Indirect Procurement" Article:**](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
      - [Ready for publication.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
      - [**Blocker:** Requires Framer access for the `pod zero` account.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
      - [**Action:** Ben to grant access.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
      - [**Method:** Replace the existing blog post with the new content.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0)

### Content Feedback & Refinements

  - [**Color Scheme:** Sydney to verify all images use the correct hex values for the LightSource orange/red, which appears differently across screens.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=237.0)
  - [**Writing Style:** Reduce repetitive, "punchy" sentences (e.g., three-word sentences) for better readability.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=423.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This style is effective for emphasis but becomes tiring when overused.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=423.0)
  - [**Source Linking:** Add direct links to analyst firm reports.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Supports claims and builds relationships with analysts.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)

### GrowthX's Agentic Content Process

  - [Aida detailed GrowthX's agentic pipeline, built on the Atlas platform.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [**Key Steps:**](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
    1.  [**Outline Control:** Human-controlled outlines ensure logical flow and topic relevance.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
    2.  [**Research:** Uses advanced tools (Exa, Tavoli) to generate and answer questions.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
    3.  [**Fact-Checking:** An agent verifies claims against sources, flagging unverified statements.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=950.0)
    4.  [**Human Fine-Tuning:** The process delivers content that is 80–90% complete.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1028.0)

### LLM Monitoring & Comms Alignment

  - [**Problem:** Ben's marketing team is a "one-man band" needing comms alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1176.0)
  - [**Solution:** A new "comms huddle" will align GrowthX, Gumshoe (LLM monitoring), and a new PR team.](https://fathom.video/share/Lrypv5DtLzxALetyhssxHmedvJ-xpeR7?tab=summary&timestamp=1176.0)
